
### County Level Analysis

```{r, warning = FALSE, echo = FALSE}

### Reading in data
###Desktop
#setwd("C:/Users/john/Documents/GitHub_Data/VCAP-Open-Hours-Project-Data")

###Laptop
setwd("C:/Users/johnc/Documents/GitHub_Data/VCAP-Open-Hours-Project-Data")

break_points = c(0,50000,100000, 160000)

all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours <- read_csv("vet_clinic_open_hours_cleaned.csv") %>%
  separate(col = full_census_tract, into = c("census_tract", "county", "state_name"), sep = ", ", remove = FALSE) %>% 
  mutate(NAME = paste(county, state_name, sep=", ")) %>% 
  left_join(select(us_counties_dat, NAME, population_estimate, median_income_estimate), by = "NAME") %>% 
  mutate(income_group = cut(median_income_estimate, breaks = break_points, include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE, labels = c("Low Income", "Medium Income", "High Income")))


county_level_dat <- read_csv("vet_clinics_open_hours_by_county.csv") %>% 
  mutate(income_group = cut(median_income_estimate, breaks = break_points, include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE, labels = c("Low Income", "Medium Income", "High Income")),
         has_clinic = case_when(total_vet_clinics == 0 ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1))

county_level_dat_with_SVI <- read_csv("vet_clinics_open_hours_with_SVI_by_county.csv") %>% 
  mutate(income_group = cut(median_income_estimate, breaks = break_points, include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE, labels = c("Low Income", "Medium Income", "High Income")),
         has_clinic = case_when(total_vet_clinics == 0 ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1))

## Creating functions



```

#### County Level Datatable

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}


df <- data.frame(column_name = NULL, min = NULL, mean = NULL, median = NULL, max = NULL, SD = NULL)

for (i in names(county_level_dat)){
  
  
 if (class(county_level_dat[[i]]) == "numeric"){
   
   five_number_stats <- county_level_dat_with_SVI %>% 
    mutate(column_name = i) %>% 
    group_by(column_name) %>% 
    summarise(min = min(county_level_dat[[i]]),
            mean = round(mean(county_level_dat[[i]]),2),
            median = median(county_level_dat[[i]]),
            max = max(county_level_dat[[i]]),
            SD = round(sd(county_level_dat[[i]])),2)
   
   df <- rbind(df, five_number_stats)
   

 }
  

}

summary_stats <- df %>% 
  select(-"2")

datatable(summary_stats)

```
#### Income Group Table

```{r, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
income_groups <- county_level_dat %>% 
  group_by(income_group) %>% 
  summarise(min = min(median_income_estimate),
            max = max(median_income_estimate))
 
table(county_level_dat$income_group)

income_groups

```


#### Percentages for Type of Care and Clinic Type

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

prop.table(table(all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$income_group, all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$clinic_type_text), margin = 1)
prop.table(table(all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$income_group, all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$clinic_type), margin = 1)

```

#### Type of Care Chi Square with Income Group and Cramer V

```{r, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
chisq.test(table(all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$income_group, all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$clinic_type_text))
cramerV(table(all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$income_group, all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$clinic_type_text))


```


#### Clinic Type Chi Square with Income Group and Cramer V

```{r, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
chisq.test(table(all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$income_group, all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$clinic_type))
cramerV(table(all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$income_group, all_clinics_cleaned_with_hours$clinic_type))

```








```{r, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}

income_groups <- county_level_dat %>% 
  group_by(income_group) %>% 
  summarise(min = min(median_income_estimate),
            max = max(median_income_estimate))

prop_table <- prop.table(table(county_level_dat$income_group, county_level_dat$has_clinic), margin = 1)

chi_test_result <- chisq.test(table(county_level_dat$income_group, county_level_dat$has_clinic))

cram_v_result <- cramerV(table(county_level_dat$income_group, county_level_dat$has_clinic))

# table(county_level_dat$income_group, county_level_dat$total_vet_clinics)
# 
# table(county_level_dat$income_group)
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = median_income_estimate))+
#   geom_histogram(binwidth = 10000, color = "black")
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = median_income_estimate, y = `Farm/Large Animal`)) +
#   geom_point()
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = income_group, y = `General/Primary`)) +
#   geom_boxplot()
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = income_group, y = `Emergency Care`)) +
#   geom_boxplot()
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = income_group, y = `Low Cost`)) +
#   geom_boxplot()
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = income_group, y = `Mobile/Home Care`)) +
#   geom_boxplot()
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = income_group, y = `Farm/Large Animal`)) +
#   geom_boxplot()
# 
# ggplot(data = county_level_dat, aes(x = income_group, y = `Specialty Care`)) +
#   geom_boxplot()


income_group_by_stats <- county_level_dat_with_SVI %>% 
  group_by(income_group) %>% 
  summarise(median_hours = median(total_open_hours),
            total_clinics = round(sum(total_vet_clinics), 2),
            total_area = round(sum(AREA_SQMI), 1),
            median_pop = median(population_estimate)) %>% 
  mutate(clinics_per_100SQMI = round((total_clinics/total_area), 4)*100)

income_group_states <- county_level_dat_with_SVI %>% 
  group_by(income_group, state_name) %>% 
  summarise(count = n()) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = income_group, values_from = count) %>% 
  replace_na(list(`Low Income` = 0, `High Income` = 0, `Medium Income` = 0)) %>% 
  mutate(low_income_percent = round(`Low Income`/sum(`Low Income`), 4)*100,
         medium_income_percent = round(`Medium Income`/sum(`Medium Income`), 4)*100,
         high_income_percent = round(`High Income`/sum(`High Income`), 4)*100)

ggplot(data = county_level_dat_with_SVI, aes(x = E_TOTPOP))+
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10000)

# ggplot(county_level_dat_with_SVI, aes(x = total_open_hours)) + 
#   geom_histogram(binwidth = 1) + 
#   xlim(-1,150)
# 
# ggplot(county_level_dat_with_SVI, aes(x = total_open_hours, y = EP_HBURD))+
#   geom_point()+
#   xlim(1,20000)
#   
# cor(x = county_level_dat_with_SVI$M_HU, county_level_dat_with_SVI$EP_HBURD)

```

#### Income Group and Whether County has Clinic Chi Square and Cramer V

```{r, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}

prop_table

chi_test_result

cram_v_result

```

#### Income Group Interesting Stats DT and Percent in Each State

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

datatable(income_group_by_stats)

datatable(income_group_states)

```
### Findings/Story
One of the stories I am seeing here is that while the type of clinic and type of care does not vary too much between the income groups, Almost all high income counties have a clinic while only 70% of the low income counties have a clinic. One has to travel a lot farther to get to a clinic than if one is living in a high income county. I performed a chi square test and a cramer v test on income group and type of care, type of clinics, and whether the county has a clinic or not. While all three were statistically significant, both type of care and type of clinic almost had no association between income group and type of care or type of clinic. However, income group and whether the county has at least one clinic had a cramer v score of 0.2 which I would classify as at least a weak association. 

A large part of this is because higher income counties tend to have a denser population. With that being said, If the goal is for everyone, regardless of where they live, have access to a clinic, it is important that we understand what areas are showing a lack vet clinics and what types of clinics are they missing. Are there other ways of offering access to vet care to lower income and lower densely populated areas beside building clinics there. Perhaps setting up more mobile/at home clinics for basic care.

I believe there are recent articles explaining that lower income groups have a harder time paying for vet care. It is unfortunate these lower income areas also need to travel farther to access a vet clinic.


### In Progress...

#### Multiple Linear Regression and Logisical Regression

#### Variables for Multiple Linear Regression
```{r, echo=FALSE, warning = FALSE, include = FALSE}
#Income Group is colinear
mlr_raw_dat <- county_level_dat_with_SVI %>% 
  select(total_open_hours, population_estimate, M_HU, E_MOBILE, EP_HBURD, total_vet_clinics)



# Combining the encoded variables back with the original data frame
final_df <- mlr_raw_dat %>%
  filter(total_vet_clinics != 0) %>% 
  select(-total_vet_clinics)

library(rsample)
set.seed(123) # for reproducibility
split <- initial_split(final_df, prop = 0.80)
train_set <- training(split)
test_set <- testing(split)
#pairs(final_df)

model_1 <- lm(total_open_hours ~ population_estimate + M_HU + E_MOBILE + EP_HBURD, data = train_set)

predictions <- predict(model_1, newdata = test_set)

mae <- mean(abs(test_set$total_open_hours - predictions))

car::vif(model_1)

```
